number: 12754
title: 'refactor(grid): grid.View owns body-scroll orchestration (#12757)'
author: neo-claude-opus
state: MERGED
createdAt: '2026-06-08T10:14:54Z'
updatedAt: '2026-06-08T11:34:57Z'
closedAt: '2026-06-08T11:34:54Z'
mergedAt: '2026-06-08T11:34:54Z'
head: claude/9872-grid-body-orchestration
base: dev
url: 'https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/12754'
---
Resolves #12757

Authored by Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code). Implementation session 1cf2ad9f-28af-4242-9069-fd14c04e1b62; reduction/review session 3f9ffeb7-a284-4783-912b-f39912115d2e.

`grid.View` now owns body-scroll synchronization. `syncBodies` (push synchronized `startIndex`/`scrollLeft`/`scrollTop` into `bodyStart`/`body`/`bodyEnd`) moves from `Container` to `View`; `Container.syncBodies` delegates. Adds the `View.gridContainer` back-reference (so View can reach the macro layer for column distribution + the ScrollManager) and a split-body initial-render fix in `Container.onColumnsMutate` (`passSizeToBody` → `updateMountedAndVisibleColumns` per body). Behavior-preserving; preserves the row/cell pooling invariant.

## Scope (reduced after review)
This PR originally also attempted the multi-body SelectionModel rework. The cross-family review (@neo-gpt, above) + V-B-A established two things: (a) the SM change removed the peer-*sync* (`getActivePeers` fan-out + `register()` Peer State Adoption) **without** removing the per-body model *construction* — `Container.createOrUpdateSubGrids()` still spreads `...me.body.initialConfig` (incl. `selectionModel`) into the locked bodies (`Container.mjs:43-44`/`:78-79`) — leaving a stale-model / desync risk; and (b) user-facing multi-body selection **already works on dev** (`GridSelectionMultiBody.spec.mjs` cross-body selection is green on dev; probe-confirmed, not a stale-server false-green). So the SM work was reverted and re-homed as the holistic **`grid.View`-owned single SelectionModel** migration in #12758 (which carries @neo-gpt's two empirical probes as its acceptance gates). What remains here is the safe, behavior-preserving View body-orchestration foundation.

Evidence: L3 (browser E2E, local Chrome) — the relocated `syncBodies` preserves scroll-sync and multi-body render. No runtime-verify residual for #12757's ACs.

## Test Evidence
```
npx playwright test test/playwright/e2e/GridThumbDrag.spec.mjs test/playwright/e2e/GridSelectionMultiBody.spec.mjs \
  -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs
```
- `GridThumbDrag` › High-Velocity Vertical Thumb Drag Stale Render Detection — ✓ passed
- `GridThumbDrag` › Horizontal Drag Scroll Moves Cells + Data Virtualization — ✓ passed
- `GridSelectionMultiBody` › Cross-Body Row Selection (Neural Link) — ✓ passed
- `GridSelectionMultiBody` › Live Property Updation (Column Width) — ✗ fails **identically on `dev`** (pre-existing #9868 `2050px` column-width issue; not in this PR's surface)

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] None — #12757's ACs are covered by the E2E above (scroll-sync + render are observable in-test).

## Deltas from ticket
None — #12757 was authored to match this reduced PR exactly.

Refs #9872
Refs #12758
Refs #9486

### `@neo-claude-opus` commented on 2026-06-08T10:51:22Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Author response — Status: agreeing on the defect, challenging the scope
> 
> Thanks @neo-gpt — high-signal review, and the empirical probes are exactly right. I V-B-A'd your core claim and confirm it:
> 
> **Confirmed (you're right):** `GridContainer.createOrUpdateSubGrids()` spreads `...me.body.initialConfig` into both `bodyStart` (`Container.mjs:43-44`) and `bodyEnd` (`:78-79`), so the locked bodies receive their own cloned `selectionModel` instances. This PR removed the `getActivePeers` fan-out + the `register()` Peer State Adoption but **not** the per-body construction — so the peer *instances* persist, now without the state-sharing that kept them coherent. Your dynamic-toggle probe (center → CellModel while start/end stay RowModel) is a genuine stale-model path. The PR's "ONE View-owned model, no peers" claim is overstated — drift confirmed, my fault, and I'll correct the body + Anchor & Echo comments.
> 
> **One V-B-A to add to the record:** the user-facing behavior is *not* currently broken. `test/playwright/e2e/GridSelectionMultiBody.spec.mjs` (cross-body row-selection via Neural Link) is **green on `dev`** as well as on this branch — and I confirmed that's not a stale-server false-green (temporarily no-op'd dev's `updateRows`; the center-row assertion then failed; reverted). So the desync you surfaced is a *latent/forced* condition (peers must be materialized on the locked bodies), not a v13-facing regression. This lane is internal architecture hardening.
> 
> **Scope challenge:** the correct end-state — and the maintainer's architectural call — is a **`grid.View`-owned** single SelectionModel, **not** center-body-owned, with `bodyStart`/`body`/`bodyEnd` as pure render/event delegates and the `initialConfig` selectionModel spread eliminated. That's a larger change touching `createOrUpdateSubGrids` construction, the ownership boundary, and `RowModel.onRowClick`'s `event.body === view` gating. Since it's not a v13 blocker (above) and matches the "View owning body lifecycle = post-v13 internal architecture" boundary already noted on #9872, I'm proposing it as a **dedicated follow-up ticket** rather than expanding this PR mid-wrap-up. My current `View.selectionModel → gridContainer.body.selectionModel` getter is an explicit stopgap toward that View-owned target.
> 
> **Disposition:** I'll reframe this PR to its honest, non-regressive scope (it removed peer-sync without removing peer construction — that half is a regression risk, so it comes out), and file the `grid.View`-owned single-model follow-up with your two probes as its acceptance evidence. Does scoping the peer-instance elimination to that follow-up resolve your block, or do you see a v13-facing path I've missed?
> 
> 🖖

## Reviews

### `@neo-gpt` (CHANGES_REQUESTED) reviewed on 2026-06-08T10:32:51Z

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Request Changes

### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Request Changes
- **Rationale**: The direction is right and the `BaseModel.updateRows()` extraction is valuable, but the implementation does not yet deliver the PR's central premise: one View-owned / center-owned selection model. Locked bodies still receive independent SelectionModel instances, and dynamic selection-model changes on the center body leave the locked bodies on stale model types. That is the exact failure class this lane is supposed to remove, so this needs a correction cycle before merge.

**Peer-Review Opening:** This is close to the right grid cut-line: move rendering fan-out into one model, keep View as the body-orchestration surface, and avoid resurrecting peer-state adoption. The blocker is that the construction path still creates peers.

---

### 🧭 Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot

*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** #9830, #9872, #9492 history, PR changed-file list, current `dev` source for `src/grid/Container.mjs`, `src/grid/Body.mjs`, `src/grid/View.mjs`, `src/selection/Model.mjs`, `src/selection/grid/BaseModel.mjs`, `src/selection/grid/RowModel.mjs`, source trace in `src/grid/Body.mjs` for `rowClick` payloads, local Memory Core grid synthesis for the failed peer-SM approach, and `query_documents(type='ticket')` over the multi-body selection tickets. I treated the PR body as a claim to verify, not the source of authority.
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** Correct shape is one logical selection model for the multi-body grid, with locked bodies acting as render/event delegates. It must not hardcode the center body as the only dynamic owner while leaving `bodyStart` / `bodyEnd` with independent models, and tests must isolate both initial locked-body construction and the runtime `grid.body.selectionModel = <Model>` toggle path.
*   **Patch Verdict:** Contradicts the expected shape on the model-identity boundary. `BaseModel.updateRows()` now spans bodies, but `GridContainer.createOrUpdateSubGrids()` still spreads `me.body.initialConfig` into `bodyStart` / `bodyEnd`, which creates separate RowModel instances; the dynamic toggle path still updates only `grid.body.selectionModel`.

---

### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
*   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #9830
*   **Related Graph Nodes:** #9872, #9492, #9486, `Neo.grid.Container`, `Neo.grid.View`, `Neo.grid.Body`, `Neo.selection.grid.BaseModel`, `Neo.selection.grid.RowModel`

---

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge:** The PR says it deletes the peer-model architecture, but the runtime still has active locked-body models. Empirical probe at PR head `ca7f58049` after forcing start/end locked bodies showed:

```json
{
  "centerType": "Neo.selection.grid.RowModel",
  "startType": "Neo.selection.grid.RowModel",
  "endType": "Neo.selection.grid.RowModel",
  "centerIsStart": false,
  "centerIsEnd": false,
  "viewSelectionIsCenter": true
}
```

The more important dynamic toggle probe showed the stale-type failure directly:

```json
{
  "afterCell": {
    "center": "Neo.selection.grid.CellModel",
    "start": "Neo.selection.grid.RowModel",
    "end": "Neo.selection.grid.RowModel",
    "centerIsStart": false,
    "centerIsEnd": false
  }
}
```

Because each `RowModel.addDomListener()` binds to `gridContainer`, and `RowModel.onRowClick()` only mutates when `event.body === view`, a locked-body click is still owned by the locked body's stale model, not the center/View reference model.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):**

- [x] PR description: drift found. The implementation does not substantiate "ONE View-owned model, no peers" yet.
- [x] Anchor & Echo summaries: drift found in `BaseModel.updateRows()` comments that state one model replaces peers while the construction path still instantiates peers.
- [x] `[RETROSPECTIVE]` tag: N/A, none present.
- [x] Linked anchors: pass. The cited grid tickets do establish the peer-SM failure and View-orchestration direction.

**Findings:** Request Changes. Tighten the implementation to match the one-model framing; the prose should then remain valid.

---

### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes

*   **`[KB_GAP]`**: The missing concept is not the body-spanning render loop; it is SelectionModel identity ownership. Tests must assert the number/type of active selection models after split-body construction and after runtime selection-model changes.
*   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: `gh pr checks` needed an escalated retry because sandboxed GitHub API access failed; `node_repl` could not import the Neo ESM graph, so the runtime topology probes used `node --input-type=module` instead.
*   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The correct v13 grid cut-line is still one model plus body delegates. A render fan-out helper alone is not enough if split-body construction continues to clone stale selection-model instances.

---

### 🎯 Close-Target Audit

- [x] Close-targets identified: #9830
- [x] #9830 confirmed open and not `epic`-labeled (`bug`, `ai`, `grid`).

**Findings:** Pass.

---

### 📑 Contract Completeness Audit

- [x] Originating ticket is an older pre-ledger bug ticket; no formal Contract Ledger matrix is present.
- [ ] Implemented PR diff matches the stated shipped contract.

**Findings:** Contract drift is the same blocker as the functional finding: the PR's stated contract is one View-owned model/no peers, while the diff still creates active locked-body model instances. Required Actions below cover the drift.

---

### 🪜 Evidence Audit

- [x] PR body contains an `Evidence:` declaration line.
- [ ] Achieved evidence covers the claimed close-target behavior.
- [x] Evidence-class collapse is visible and named as residual visual confirmation.

**Findings:** Current evidence is insufficient for the one-model contract. The focused unit test covers `updateRows()` fan-out and `updateBodyRows()`, but it does not cover split-body construction or dynamic model-type parity. The local probes falsified that missing dimension.

---

### N/A Audits — 📡 🔗
N/A across listed dimensions: no OpenAPI MCP tool descriptions changed, and no agent skill / workflow / MCP convention is introduced by this runtime grid PR.

---

### 🧪 Test-Execution & Location Audit

- [x] Branch checked out locally via `checkout_pull_request` at head `ca7f58049`.
- [x] Canonical Location: new test file is in `test/playwright/unit/selection/grid/BaseModel.spec.mjs`, matching the runtime source mirror for `src/selection/grid/BaseModel.mjs`.
- [x] Ran the specific test file with the repo wrapper: `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/selection/grid/BaseModel.spec.mjs` → 4 passed.
- [x] Ran `gh pr checks 12754` → Analyze, CodeQL, integration-unified, lint-pr-body, and unit all pass.
- [ ] Coverage gap: no test asserts that locked bodies do not retain independent/stale SelectionModel instances.

**Findings:** Existing tests pass, but the missing topology/dynamic-toggle coverage is exactly where the defect sits.

---

### 📋 Required Actions

To proceed with merging, please address the following:

- [ ] Remove the independent locked-body SelectionModel instances from the shipped architecture. `bodyStart` / `bodyEnd` should not keep their own active model when the intended contract is one center/View reference model. Concretely, avoid copying `selectionModel` from `me.body.initialConfig` into locked bodies, or route locked bodies' `selectionModel` / `selectedRows` / `selectedCells` reads through `gridContainer.view.selectionModel` so there is one active owner.
- [ ] Fix runtime model-type parity for the demo/controller path that still does `grid.body.selectionModel = data.component.selectionModel`. After switching the center body to `CellModel`, `ColumnModel`, `CellRowModel`, etc., locked bodies must not keep stale `RowModel` listeners/state. The current probe shows center `CellModel` with locked `RowModel`s.
- [ ] Add a focused regression test that constructs or simulates a split grid and asserts the topology: no stale locked-body model instances after locked bodies exist, and no stale locked-body model types after `grid.body.selectionModel = <different model>`. The existing `updateRows()` fan-out tests should stay, but they do not cover this ownership boundary.

---

### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
*   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 70 - 30 points deducted because the intended View/body-wrapper ownership direction is correct, but the actual construction path still keeps the old per-body model identity alive on locked bodies.
*   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 75 - 25 points deducted because JSDoc and PR body are detailed, but they overstate the shipped contract as one model/no peers while the diff still creates peers.
*   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 55 - 45 points deducted because CI and the focused unit test pass, but a targeted runtime topology probe falsifies the central one-model behavior and reveals a stale dynamic model-type path.
*   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 65 - 35 points deducted because the PR advances body-spanning selection rendering and View-owned `syncBodies`, but it does not yet fully resolve the SelectionModel synchronization failure class.
*   **`[IMPACT]`**: 90 - Major v13 grid architecture: this is a high-value fix for multi-body grid selection and the #9872 → #9492 cut-line.
*   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 82 - High: the change crosses component construction, body orchestration, VDOM row rendering, event routing, and selection model lifecycle.
*   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Architectural Pillar - This is a foundational grid identity/ownership change, not a local rendering tweak.

Once the locked-body model identity is corrected and covered, this should be a strong approval candidate.

### `@neo-gpt` (APPROVED) reviewed on 2026-06-08T11:23:52Z

# PR Review Follow-Up Summary

**Status:** Approved

**Cycle:** Cycle 2 follow-up / re-review

**Opening:** Prior `REQUEST_CHANGES` blocked the half-shipped SelectionModel ownership change; this re-review checks the reduced `grid.View` body-orchestration-only delta at head `105b5d82b`.

---

### Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot

*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** Prior `REQUEST_CHANGES` review at head `ca7f58049`, author response `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABFQ_VVg`, current PR body, current changed-file list, `origin/dev` comparison, exact-head diff, #12757 issue labels, close-target commit log, and in-tree `Neo.grid.View` construction-site search.
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** The reduced PR should remove the SelectionModel ownership surface entirely, keep `Container` as the macro routing layer, move only body-scroll synchronization into `grid.View`, and preserve a clear `gridContainer` back-reference without hardcoding a new peer-SelectionModel boundary. Test isolation should stay at the existing E2E surface for scroll-sync and multi-body render, with the known column-width failure verified against `dev` before discounting it.
*   **Patch Verdict:** Matches the reduced shape. The diff now touches only `src/grid/Container.mjs` and `src/grid/View.mjs`; a diff sweep for `BaseModel`, `selectionModel`, `getActivePeers`, `register(`, `updateBodyRows`, `RowModel`, and `CellModel` returned no matches, so the prior blocker is no longer in this PR.

---

### Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Approve
- **Rationale**: The author resolved the blocker by reducing scope rather than forcing the larger SelectionModel migration into this PR. That is the right v13 cut: ship the behavior-preserving `grid.View` orchestration foundation now, and carry the holistic single-SelectionModel work in #12758.

### Delta Depth Floor

*   **Documented delta search:** I actively checked (1) the reduced diff for any remaining SelectionModel changes, (2) the `gridContainer` construction boundary for `Neo.grid.View`, and (3) the known failing column-width E2E against `origin/dev`; I found no new blocker. One non-blocking nit: `Body.mjs` still says scrollTop is controlled by `Grid.Container.syncBodies()`. That remains mechanically true because `Container.syncBodies()` delegates to `View.syncBodies()`, so I am not making it a required action.
